# **Computer Science (Algorithms and Data Structures)**

## **Data Structures**

* Arrays are L1 contiguous memory blocks storing L0 elements accessible by index | Structure | 0.97  
* Linked Lists are L1 linear collections of L0 data elements where each element points to the next | Structure | 0.96  
* Stacks (LIFO) and Queues (FIFO) are L1 abstract data types governed by specific access rules | Logic | 0.97  
* Hash Tables use a hash function to map L0 keys to L1 buckets, enabling ![][image1] average time complexity | Retrieval | 0.96  
* Binary Trees are L1 hierarchical structures where each node has at most two children | Structure | 0.95  
* Graphs represent L1 networks of L0 nodes (vertices) and connections (edges) | Systems | 0.96

## **Algorithms and Complexity**

* Big O Notation is the L1 mathematical framework for describing the upper bound of L1 algorithm complexity | Metrics | 0.98  
* Sorting Algorithms (e.g., Quicksort, Mergesort) organize L0 data into a specific L1 order | Methodology | 0.96  
* Searching Algorithms (e.g., Binary Search) locate L0 data within L1 structures | Methodology | 0.97  
* Recursion is a method of solving a problem where the L1 solution depends on solutions to smaller instances | Logic | 0.95  
* Dynamic Programming is an L1 optimization method that solves complex problems by breaking them into overlapping subproblems | Mathematics | 0.94

## **CS Meta-Context**

* P vs NP is the primary L3 open question in Computer Science regarding L1 computational complexity | Philosophy of Science | 0.99  
* Algorithms are L1 "Recipes" for processing L0 Information Theory signals | Information Theory | 0.97  
* Garbage Collection is a metabolic L1 process that automatically reclaims memory from L0 objects no longer in use | System Metabolism | 0.95  
* Abstraction Layers (from binary to high-level code) represent the L1 to L5 progression of the Kitbash Stack | Architecture | 0.96
